title: coptophyllum bracteatum
intro: Coptophyllum bracteatum, also known as the "Australian coral tree", is a flowering plant native to Australia and New Guinea, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Coptophyllum bracteatum include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the plant are typically dried and used in infusions or teas to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
